predictions.question: Which counties have the highest number of customer outages reported at one time?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?county (MAX(?n) AS ?maxOutages) WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:numberOfOutages ?n .
}
GROUP BY ?county
ORDER BY DESC(?maxOutages)
LIMIT 10

sparql_interpretations.label: Peak single-snapshot outages per county
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "at one time" reads as the single highest instantaneous outage record per county.
sparql_interpretations.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?county (MAX(?n) AS ?maxOutages) WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:numberOfOutages ?n .
}
GROUP BY ?county
ORDER BY DESC(?maxOutages)
LIMIT 10

sparql_interpretations.label: Total accumulated outages per county
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "highest number" could instead mean the largest summed outage volume aggregated across all records for a county.
sparql_interpretations.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?county (SUM(?n) AS ?totalOutages) WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:numberOfOutages ?n .
}
GROUP BY ?county
ORDER BY DESC(?totalOutages)
LIMIT 10

PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>

predictions.question: Which customer outage records were recorded in the calendar year after 2021?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?rec ?date WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:recordDateTime ?date .
  FILTER(?date >= "2022-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?date <  "2023-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
LIMIT 10

sparql_interpretations.label: Exactly the year 2022
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "the calendar year after 2021" most literally denotes the single following year, 2022.
sparql_interpretations.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?rec ?date WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:recordDateTime ?date .
  FILTER(?date >= "2022-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?date <  "2023-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
LIMIT 10

sparql_interpretations.label: Any year strictly after 2021
sparql_interpretations.rationale: It may instead mean all records dated in any calendar year later than 2021 (2022 onward).
sparql_interpretations.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?rec ?date WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:recordDateTime ?date .
  FILTER(?date >= "2022-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
LIMIT 10

predictions.question: Which nighttime-light images cover the Orlando-area county in 2025?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?image WHERE {
  ?image a goo:NTLImage ;
         goo:representsCounty ?county ;
         goo:acquisitionDateTime ?dt .
  ?county goo:fipsCode "12095" .
  FILTER(?dt >= "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?dt <  "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
LIMIT 10

PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>

  FILTER(?dt >= "2017-08-15T20:15:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&

}

predictions.question: Which jurisdiction is represented by the first alphabetical order county in Florida on the 10th of the 3rd month in the last year of the 2010s?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?county WHERE {
  ?county a goo:County ;
          goo:locatedInState ?state ;
          rdfs:label ?cname .
  ?state goo:statePostalCode "FL" .
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:recordDateTime ?dt .
  FILTER(?dt >= "2019-03-10T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?dt <  "2019-03-11T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
ORDER BY ASC(?cname)
LIMIT 1

predictions.question: How many outages were recorded in the customer outage record for the Florida county with the seat in Bronson, on August 15, 2017 at 20:15?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?numOutages WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:numberOfOutages ?numOutages ;
       goo:recordDateTime ?dt .
  ?county goo:fipsCode "12075" .
  FILTER(?dt >= "2017-08-15T20:15:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?dt <  "2017-08-15T20:16:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Which nighttime-light scene corresponds to the customer outage record for the Florida county with the seat in Bronson, on August 15, 2017 at 20:15?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?image WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:recordDateTime ?recDt .
  ?county goo:fipsCode "12075" .
  FILTER(?recDt >= "2017-08-15T20:15:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?recDt <  "2017-08-15T20:16:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
  ?image a goo:NTLImage ;
         goo:representsCounty ?county ;
         goo:acquisitionDateTime ?imgDt .
  FILTER(SUBSTR(STR(?imgDt), 1, 10) = "2017-08-15")
}
LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Which outage map corresponds to the customer outage record for the Florida county with the largest city being Williston, on August 15, 2017 at 20:15?
Which severity prediction map was derived from the nighttime-light scene for Jacksonville's county in 2020, the 21st night of September?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?map WHERE {
  ?map a goo:OutageMap ;
       goo:generatedFromNTLImage ?image .
  ?image a goo:NTLImage ;
         goo:representsCounty ?county ;
         goo:acquisitionDateTime ?dt .
  ?county goo:fipsCode "12031" .
  FILTER(?dt >= "2020-09-21T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?dt <  "2020-09-22T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Which sensor captured any random NTL image?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>

         goo:fromSensor ?sensor .

predictions.question: During the Hurricane Ian disruption period, which outage records for the southwest Florida county centered on Fort Myers were above 100?

sparql_interpretations.label: Full Hurricane Ian event window
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "disruption period" is read as the hurricane event's own start-to-end interval.

sparql_interpretations.label: Florida landfall day only (2022-09-28)
sparql_interpretations.rationale: The disruption period could be narrowed to Ian's Florida landfall calendar day.
sparql_interpretations.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?rec ?n WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:numberOfOutages ?n ;
       goo:recordDateTime ?dt .
  ?county goo:fipsCode "12071" .
  FILTER(?n > 100)
  FILTER(?dt >= "2022-09-28T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?dt <  "2022-09-29T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
ORDER BY DESC(?n)
LIMIT 10

predictions.question: During the Hurricane Ian landfall date, which nighttime-light scenes line up with outage snapshots for the southwest Florida county centered on Fort Myers?
Which severity maps correspond to the southwest Florida county centered on Fort Myers around Hurricane Ian?

sparql_interpretations.label: Hurricane Ian full event window
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "around Hurricane Ian" is read as the event's start-to-end interval.

sparql_interpretations.label: Ian landfall day only
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "around" could instead be narrowed to the single landfall calendar day.
sparql_interpretations.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?map WHERE {
  ?map a goo:OutageMap ;
       goo:generatedFromNTLImage ?image .
  ?image a goo:NTLImage ;
         goo:representsCounty ?county ;
         goo:acquisitionDateTime ?dt .
  ?county goo:fipsCode "12071" .
  FILTER(SUBSTR(STR(?dt), 1, 10) = "2022-09-28")
}
LIMIT 10

predictions.question: For each county, what is the maximum number of customer outages recorded in the year Hurricane Irma occurred in?
For each second-level administrative division in the U.S., what is their average social vulnerability index?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?county (AVG(?svi) AS ?avgSvi) WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:SVIRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:sviOverallScore ?svi .
}
GROUP BY ?county
LIMIT 10

predictions.question: On what date and time did each jurisdiction reach its maximum recorded number of customer outages?

PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>

PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

predictions.question: Which U.S. county is depicted in the power outage severity map corresponding to a specific customer outage record?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?county WHERE {
  ?map a goo:OutageMap ;
       goo:generatedFromNTLImage ?image .
  ?image goo:representsCounty ?county .
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county .
}
LIMIT 1

predictions.question: What is the maximum number of power outages recorded for the county in the third most populous state named after Andrew Jackson in the Eagle-I dataset?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT (MAX(?n) AS ?maxOutages) WHERE {
  ?rec a goo:CustomerOutageRecord ;
       goo:representsCounty ?county ;
       goo:numberOfOutages ?n .
  ?county goo:fipsCode "12063" .
}

predictions.question: Is there an EAGLE-I snapshot for the Tampa, Florida county that also has a matching NTL scene?
For the central-Florida county where the largest city is named Clermont, what derived severity maps are associated with the VIIRS-captured scene for the month of March in 2024?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?map WHERE {
  ?map a goo:OutageMap ;
       goo:generatedFromNTLImage ?image .
  ?image a goo:NTLImage ;
         goo:representsCounty ?county ;
         goo:fromSensor ?sensor ;
         goo:acquisitionDateTime ?dt .
  ?county goo:fipsCode "12069" .
  ?sensor rdfs:label ?sname .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?sname), "VIIRS"))
  FILTER(?dt >= "2024-03-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime &&
         ?dt <  "2024-04-01T00:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)
}
LIMIT 10

predictions.question: How many tropical cyclones were active on the date 6 October 2024 in the Atlantic basin, and what were their names and maximum sustained wind speeds?

On Ian's Florida landfall day, what was the peak outage count in the southwest Florida county centered on Fort Myers, and were any hurricanes active within the general area of the county that day, and if so what were their names?

sparql_interpretations.label: Hurricanes within ~100 km of the county that day
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "within the general area of the county" is read as a proximity buffer around the county geometry.

sparql_interpretations.label: Any hurricane with a track fix that day
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "general area" could be loosened to any tropical cyclone active on the landfall day regardless of distance.

predictions.question: On the day of the Moore tornado, how many tornado records occurred in or within the general area of Cleveland County, Oklahoma, and what were their EF ratings and injury counts?

sparql_interpretations.label: Cleveland County plus neighboring counties
sparql_interpretations.rationale: "in or within the general area" is read as the county together with its bordering counties.

sparql_interpretations.label: Strictly within Cleveland County
sparql_interpretations.rationale: It may instead be limited to tornado records occurring inside the county itself.

predictions.question: What is the difference between how many storm events were recorded in each state between the year the COVID pandemic started and one decade earlier?
How many and which hurricanes made landfall in Louisiana in the month of August across all years, and when did they make landfall?
What is the ratio of tornadoes to hurricanes to thunderstorms to wildfires in Florida and California?
predictions.sparql_query:
PREFIX goo: <https://ucf-henat.github.io/GeoOutageOnto/#>
PREFIX geosparql: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX geo: <http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX units: <http://www.opengis.net/def/uom/OGC/1.0/>

SELECT ?type (COUNT(?e) AS ?cnt) WHERE {
  ?e a goo:StormEventRecord ;
     goo:occurredInState ?state ;
     goo:stormEventType ?type .
  ?state goo:statePostalCode ?pc .
  FILTER(?pc IN ("FL", "CA"))
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?type), "tornado") ||
         CONTAINS(LCASE(?type), "hurricane") ||
         CONTAINS(LCASE(?type), "thunderstorm") ||
         CONTAINS(LCASE(?type), "wildfire"))
}
GROUP BY ?type
ORDER BY DESC(?cnt)
